data.title: Solana network inflows surge — Will SOL price follow?
data.description:
Over the past 30 days, crypto market participants have bridged more than $120 million in liquidity to Solanafrom other blockchains, signaling renewed confidence in the network. Traders transferred the highest amount from Ethereum at $41.5 million, followed by a $37.3 million influx from Arbitrum, according to data from Debridge.

Meanwhile, users on Base, BNB Chain and Sonic moved $16 million, $14 million and $6.6 million, respectively.

The return of liquidity to Solana paints a stark contrast to the network's recent challenges. Following Argentina’s LIBRA memecoin scandal, which ensnared President Javier Milei, Solana saw investors move $485 million to other blockchains like Ethereum and BNB Chain.

The current liquidity influx to Solana coincides with the return of double-digit price rallies from memecoins as POPCAT, FARTCOIN, BONK and WIF rose 79%, 51%, 25% and 21%, respectively, over the past seven days.

However, further analysis shows the total generated fees for March coming in just under $46 million. For context, Solana’s fees peaked at over $400 million in January 2025. Currently, the total fees generated for the month of April are roughly $22 million.

Related: Spot Solana ETFs to launch in Canada this week

Solana price has a tough uphill climb ahead

From a technical perspective, Solana remains in a bearish trend on the 1-day chart. SOL must exhibit a bullish break of structure by closing a daily candle above $147 for a bullish trend shift.

Solana remains under the $140 level, with the 50-day exponential moving average (blue line) acting as a strong resistance. A bullish close above the 50-EMA would have increased the likelihood of a positive trend reversal, but SOL prices have stalled at current levels.

On a lower time frame (LTF) chart, Solana exhibited a bearish divergence between the price and relative strength index (RSI) indicator. Historically, a bearish divergence setup has signaled a correction period for Solana in 2025. SOL has experienced four bearish divergences since January, each following a price decline.

There is a strong similarity between its previous and current bearish divergence. Both setups took place after the price moved temporarily above the 50-day and 100-day EMA (blue and green line) on the 4-hour chart, eventually leading to a price drop.

Thus, it is possible that Solana could follow a similar path in the next few days. The 1-day demand zone is the immediate area of interest for a bounce between $115 and $108.

Meanwhile, in a recent X post, Glassnode reported a significant shift in Solana’s realized price distribution, with over 32 million SOL bought at the $130 level over the past few days. That is 5% of the total supply, which means the $130 level could be a strong support level in the future. The analysis added,

“Below $129, we see 18M $SOL (3%) at $117.99, while above, 27M $SOL(4.76%) sit at $144.54. In the short term, $144 could act as resistance and $117 as the lower bound of the price range, with $129 serving as the key pivot zone.”

Related: Bitcoin price recovery could be capped at $90K — Here’s why

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: IGO Raised to Hold From Sell by Canaccord Genuity >IGO.AU
Australian tech stocks track Wall Street peers lower
data.description:
** Technology stocks fall 0.4% to 2,233.4 points

** Sub-index tracks decline in Wall Street, where the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite shed 3.1% overnight after top player Nvidia tumbled on being hit by U.S government curbs on China exports

** WiseTech top loser on benchmark , down 2.6% and set for worst session since April 4

** Zip and Seek also among top losers

** YTD, AXIJ down 18.5%

data.title: PainChek to Meet with US FDA on De Novo Request for Adult Pain Assessment App
data.description:
PainChek said a meeting with the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is scheduled this month to discuss its request for de novo regulatory clearance for the PainChek Adult App, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The clearance would enable the company to enter the US healthcare market, potentially generating roughly $100 million per annum in aged care, the filing said.

Meanwhile, the company launched its PainChek Infant app on the Apple App Store, now available in Australia, according to the filing.

data.title: Moody's Updates Outlook on Helios Towers Amid Ongoing Deleveraging Efforts
data.description:
Moody's changed its outlook on the affirmed ratings of Helios Towers on Wednesday thanks to the company's ongoing deleveraging efforts.

The London-listed telecommunications tower group has a B1 long-term corporate family rating, with the outlook changed to positive from stable.

Helios Towers' positive free cash flow and strong operating performance were also cited as reasons for the outlook change.

data.title: CapitaLand Investment Utilizes Nearly 1.8 Million Treasury Shares to Settle Vested Awards
data.description:
CapitaLand Investment transferred 1,838,855 shares worth SG$5.5 million from its treasury shares to settle vested awards under its share plans, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Following the share transfer, the number of treasury shares decreased to 215,646,782 shares.

Shares of the company were up over 2% in recent trading.

data.title: Liontown Resources Target Price Cut 23% to A$0.50/Share by Canaccord Genuity>LTR.AU
Liontown Resources Raised to Hold From Sell by Canaccord Genuity >LTR.AU
Siemens Energy's Fiscal Q2 Preliminary Profit, Revenue Rise
data.description:
Siemens Energy said Wednesday that its preliminary profit and revenue for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 grew year over year.

Preliminary profit was 615 million euros, compared with 501 million euros a year ago.

The German energy company's revenue was 9.96 billion euros, compared with 8.28 billion euros earlier.

For fiscal 2025, Siemens Energy raised its comparable revenue forecast and expects it to grow between 13% and 15%, excluding currency translation and portfolio effects, compared with the previously forecasted growth of 8% to 10%. The company also anticipates net income of up to 1 billion euros.

data.title: Asian Pay Television Closes Onshore, Offshore Facilities
data.description:
Asian Pay Television completed the refinancing of its offshore and onshore facilities, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The onshore facilities have been refinanced for seven years while the offshore facilities have been refinanced for three years, extendable by another two years.

Under the agreement, around S$40 million of offshore loan will be moved back onshore to save on interest costs.

The size of the refinanced onshore facilities will be reduced by NT$2.0 billion to NT$27.5 billion, according to a previous filing.

Meanwhile, the size of refinanced offshore facilities will be reduced to S$50 million.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Energy Stocks Soar, Communication Services Sector Slips
data.description:
Energy stocks advanced past 3% at midday Thursday, with Brent crude futures closing up 1.8%, the highest levels since April 3.

Santos produced 21.9 million barrels of oil equivalent (mmboe) in the first quarter ended March 31, a 2% rise from 21.5 mmboe in the prior three-month period. It maintained its production volume guidance in the range of 90 mmboe to 97 mmboe for the current year but said its unit production cost in the range of AU$7 per barrel of oil equivalent to AU$7.50 boe will be elevated in the first half and lower in the second half of the year.

On the flip side, the communication services sector fell 0.4% as banks faced tougher regulatory challenges. Stock Indices fell on Wall Street after Nvidia flagged a hit of a billion dollars from US restrictions on exports to China.

Rent.com.au raised around AU$1.7 million through a non-renounceable entitlement issue to eligible shareholders. It will use the proceeds to establish the capability to assess customers for and to fund loans under the RentBond brand.

data.title: Market Talk Roundup: Latest on U.S. Politics
data.description:
Market Talks covering the impact of U.S. Politics and White House policies on companies and markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2321 ET - The Japan-U.S. tariff talks are likely to reach agreement soon, Citi Research's Katsuhiko Aiba says in a research report. It's highly likely that the talks will lead to an agreement within the 90-day grace period for reciprocal tariffs, the economist says. While no details were disclosed from the first round of talks, the U.S. is seeking to prioritize the negotiations with the next round of talks slated to take place within this month, Aiba says. However, even if an agreement is reached, the 10% reciprocal tariffs and sectoral tariffs will probably remain in place, the economist adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1828 ET - Kinder Morgan discloses how countries are responding to Trump's tariffs threats when it comes to U.S. natural gas exports. South Korea and Indonesia are already in specific discussions on increasing U.S. liquefied natural gas exports to improve their negotiation position regarding U.S. tariffs, CEO Kimberly Allen Dang says on a call with analysts. The European Union will also increase its use of U.S. LNG as it seems to be reluctant to ever again allow Russia to be the dominant supplier of natural gas to Europe, Dang says. In addition to growth in LNG feed gas demand, the company sees "nice upticks" in exports to Mexico, with power demand driven in part by the surge in AI and data centers and residential commercial use, the executive adds. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1815 ET - The uncertainty of tariffs, along with commodity prices, made Kinder Morgan be a little bit more conservative in communicating its outlook for the year, CEO Kimberly Allen Dang says on a call with analysts. However, the energy-infrastructure company is seeing growing natural gas demand in the U.S. It also expects any loss of the Chinese market to be more than offset by the efforts of governments in the European Union and Asia to increase the imports of U.S. liquefied natural gas to reduce trade imbalances and put themselves in a better negotiation position with regard to U.S. tariffs, Dang says. "China has not imported any U.S. liquefied natural gas since February, and yet peak gas demand is setting records," she adds. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1809 ET - Five Below shares appear to have reached a bottom after a slew of product, management and shrink problems last year and current tariff worries, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. They say the stock's valuation is at all-time lows, trading as if the company were a quarter of its size a decade ago as investors price in a worst-case scenario. But even as margins take a hit from tariffs, the company is likely to see improving revenue trends, unit base and shrink, and the allure of the store model and business position should only strengthen, the analysts say. Those tailwinds and further clarity on tariffs should help shares get a boost from their currently very cheap levels, they add. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1551 ET - Treasury Yields fall for the third consecutive day as Powell says tariffs could make it harder to decide on interest rates. "Fed Chair Powell suggested that patience was the best course," as opposed to cutting rates fast, Global X's Scott Helfstein writes. March retail sales were better than expected, while industrial production fell more than forecast. Weekly jobless claims tomorrow are expected to increase slightly, in a WSJ survey. Bond markets are set to close at 2 p.m. ET tomorrow and the whole day on Good Friday. The 10-year yield declines 0.046 percentage point to 4.276% and the two-year falls 0.043 p.p. to 3.784%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1425 ET - The Bank of Canada is likely to keep its policy interest rate unchanged for an extended period, says Bank of Nova Scotia economist Derek Holt. He argues that after Canada's April 28 election, fiscal support for the economy is likely to intensify. The two main parties, Liberals and Conservatives, are promising tax relief and financial support for trade-exposed businesses and its workers. Fiscal policy will do some of the BOC's work in terms of providing economic support, Holt says. In a trade-conflict context, it's crucial the BOC be wary of cutting too deeply, he says, arguing this could trigger a new round of inflationary pressure akin to what transpired after the Covid-19 pandemic. (paul.vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

1419 ET - The Bank of Canada decision to pause risks larger interest rate cuts later, Citi's Veronica Clark suggests. The economist says risks seem to be more clearly shifting to weaker inflation outcomes, and both of the scenarios presented by the central bank indicate Canadian growth slows and inflation is below 2% this year. With the current tariff backdrop likely to persist for at least three to six months and upcoming data set to weaken more clearly, Citi now expects a full percentage point of rate of cuts this year: a quarter point in June, half point in July and another quarter point in September. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1411 ET - Treasury yields decline as Fed Chair Powell speaks at a conference in Chicago. The monetary policymaker says tariffs could raise prices and unemployment at the same time, making decisions on interest rates harder to make. He indicates the Fed would wait for more clarity before resuming interest rate cutting. Odds of a hold in May are at 85%, up from 80% a week ago, CME data show. The 10-year yield is at 4.285% and the two-year at 3.794%, both erasing gains recorded over the past week. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1409 ET - New restrictions on selling semiconductors to China could significantly reduce the market opportunity for American chip companies, DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria tells WSJ. "While the extent of the restrictions will only be clear once the U.S. publishes the details, Nvidia's disclosure indicates the restrictions are much more severe than they were before," Luria says. Nvidia slides 9%, and is the worst performer in the S&P 500. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1353 ET - If the goal of U.S. government restrictions on Nvidia's sales of H20 chips in China was to restrict Beijing's AI capabilities, that ship has already sailed, BofA Securities analyst Vivek Arya tells WSJ. "I understand the goal is to restrict AI, but limiting this product is no way limiting China's AI capability because Huawei Technologies can make products that have similar capabilities," Arya says. The risk is that if Huawei takes its products and is able to sell them into other emerging markets and other places outside of China at a very low price, there is nothing to prevent them from doing that. And it wouldn't help the trade gap either, he adds. "It's hard to defend these moves." Nvidia slides 8%. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1339 ET - The strength in March U.S. retail sales isn't only about consumers trying to get ahead of tariffs, Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan and Shannon Grein write. Sales beat WSJ consensus in both the headline and the ex-auto figures. The report included upward revisions of previous data. Autos saw the biggest March gain, likely reflecting a rush to buy vehicles before any price increase due to tariffs. But consumers "also spent more at restaurants in March, and we're not aware of a way you can front-run tariffs with a nice night out," Quinlan and Grein say. "How consumers act in these next couple of months will be telling," they say. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1320 ET - Prologis customers are accelerating shipments, rerouting volume and have urgent demand for overflow space as they try to manage tariff volatility, says Tim Arndt, CFO of the biggest industrial REIT, in an analyst call. Free trade zones and bonded warehouses also are being evaluated. Prologis sees inventory levels rising as businesses stockpile and build resiliency, and global markets in its portfolio such as Canada, India and Brazil will become more important. Mexico stands out in terms of growing interest before and after April's tariff announcement, he says. (mark.long@wsj.com)

data.title: Hillgrove Resources Target Price Cut 20% to A$0.08/Share by Canaccord Genuity>HGO.AU
Lunnon Metals Intersects Shallow Gold Mineralization at Western Australia Gold-Nickel Project; Shares Jump 4%
data.description:
Lunnon Metals said assay results from close-spaced reverse circulation drilling at the Lady Herial gold prospect at its Kambalda gold-nickel project in Western Australia delivered widespread shallow gold mineralization, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

"Significant" intercepts from shallow depths included 13 meters at 10.3 grams per tonne (g/t) grade of gold, 27 meters at 4 g/t grade of gold, and 19 meters at 5.4 g/t grade of gold.

The program will serve as grade control drilling for potential future production purposes, the company said.

After this round of drilling, the firm will estimate an initial mineral resource, conduct open pit optimization, and submit a Mining Development and Closure Proposal to the Western Australian government, per the filing.

Lunnon Metals' shares jumped 4% in recent trading on Thursday.

data.title: Brunello Cucinelli Confirms FY25 Growth Target
data.description:
Brunello Cucinelli (BC.MI) on Wednesday updated on its guidance for 2025, confirming its expectations of a 10% growth rate and "healthy and balanced" profitability levels.

The Italian luxury fashion brand also affirmed its investment plan through 2026 to support its locally made artisanal production. For the same year, the company expects a 10% increase in turnover.

data.title: MAC Copper Target Price Cut 2.4% to A$20.50/Share by Canaccord Genuity>MAC.AU
Market Chatter: China's Q1 Smartphone Shipments Climb 3.3%
data.description:
China's smartphone shipments in the first quarter grew 3.3% year on year to 71.6 million units, Yicai Global reported Wednesday, citing data from IDC.

Chinese shipments surpassed the 1.5% growth of international smartphone shipments, the report said.

Among major smartphone brands, Xiaomi's shipments jumped the highest, up 40% from the prior year to 13.3 million units. It is followed by Huawei Technologies came in second with a 10% growth. Oppo and Vivo's shipments climbed 3.3% and 2.3%, respectively, the report said.

The shipments of US brand Apple, however, slid 9% year on year to 9.8 million units, the only brand among the top brands to drop, the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Harmony Energy Board Backs GBP210 Million Offer from Foresight Group Subsidiary
data.description:
Harmony Energy Income Trust's board agreed to a 209.9 million-pound-sterling scheme of arrangement with Foresight Group's PP Bidco.

The bidder is offering 0.924 pound per share of the British investor for battery energy storage systems, which is a 5% premium to a rival bid from Drax Group , according to a Wednesday filing.

Harmony Energy's board unanimously backed PP Bidco's all-cash offer, with the former planning to adjourn shareholder meetings for Drax's offer.

Subject to certain conditions, the scheme is expected to be completed in the second quarter and delist Harmony Energy from the London bourse's specialist fund segment.

data.title: New Zealand Job Ads Flat in Monthly Terms in March, Up 2% in Q1
data.description:
New Zealand job ads were flat in March on a monthly basis, following a 2% decline in February, data from SEEK NZ showed Thursday.

Job ads were up 2% in the three months to March compared with the preceding period, marking the dataset's first quarterly growth since August 2022. This is being perceived as a sign of a stabilizing market, the report said.

"The smaller regions recorded much of the growth month-on-month, but looking broadly at the entire quarter, most regions are demonstrating positive movement," said SEEK NZ country manager Rob Clark.

"There are early signs that economic and labor market growth may be on the horizon, albeit amid a very uncertain global trade and economic backdrop," Clark added.

Job ads grew in the cities and regional areas for the public sector, as well as in non-major city areas for the construction sector and professional services. Job ads in the consumer and industrial sectors in major cities fell.

Sandfire Resources Target Price Cut 4.5% to A$10.50/Share by Canaccord Genuity>SFR.AU
Nickel Resources Target Price Cut 31% to A$0.55/Share by Canaccord Genuity>NIC.AU
Hyundai Steel Secures ASME's Nuclear Quality Certification
data.description:
Hyundai Steel earned the American Society of Mechanical Engineers' (ASME) quality system certificate (QSC) for nuclear materials, the steelmaker said Wednesday.

ASME QSC is a global certification that verifies a supplier's materials and quality systems meet nuclear industry safety standards.

The QSC approval covers reinforced bars, section steel, and thick steel plates used in nuclear reactors, affirming the company's compliance with the global nuclear industry's quality standards.

data.title: ENS founder warns of Google spoof that tricks users with a fake subpoena
data.description:
The founder and lead developer of Ethereum Name Service has warned his X followers of an “extremely sophisticated” phishing attack that can impersonate Google and trick users into giving out login credentials. 

The phishing attack exploits Google’s infrastructure to send a fake alert to users informing them that their Google data is being shared with law enforcement due to a subpoena, ENS’ Nick Johnson said in an April 16 post to X. 

“It passes the DKIM signature check, and GMail displays it without any warnings - it even puts it in the same conversation as other, legitimate security alerts,” he said. 

As part of the attack, users are offered the chance to view the case materials or protest by clicking a support page link, which uses Google Sites, a tool that can be used to build a website on a Google subdomain, according to Johnson. 

“From there, presumably, they harvest your login credentials and use them to compromise your account; I haven’t gone further to check,” he said.

The Google domain name gives the impression it’s legit, but Johnson says there are still telltale signs it’s a phishing scam, such as the email being forwarded by a private email address. 

Scammers exploit Google systems 

In an April 11 report, software firm EasyDMARC explained that the phishing scam works by weaponizing Google Sites.

Anyone with a Google account can create a site that looks legitimate and is hosted under a trusted Google-owned domain.

They also use the Google OAuth app, where the “key trick is that you can put anything you want in the App Name field in Google,” and use a domain via Namecheap that allows them to “put no-reply@google account as From address and the reply address can be anything.”

“Finally, they forward the message to their victims. Because DKIM only verifies the message and its headers and not the envelope, the message passes signature validation and shows up as a legitimate message in the user’s inbox — even in the same thread as legit security alerts,” Johnson said. 

Google deploying countermeasures soon 

Speaking to Cointelegraph, a Google spokesperson said they are aware of the issue and are shutting down the mechanism that attackers are using to insert the “arbitrary length text,” which will prevent the method of attack from working in the future. 

“We’re aware of this class of targeted attack from the threat actor, Rockfoils, and have been rolling out protections for the past week. These protections will soon be fully deployed, which will shut down this avenue for abuse,” the spokesperson said. 

“In the meantime, we encourage users to adopt two-factor authentication and passkeys, which provide strong protection against these kinds of phishing campaigns.” 

The spokesperson added that Google will never ask for any private account credentials — including passwords, one-time passwords or push notifications, nor call users.

data.title: Market Chatter: US Pressures Allies to Join Trade Crackdown on China
data.description:
The US is leveraging tariff negotiations to pressure allies into curbing trade ties with China, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

With numerous countries seeking relief from US import taxes, US officials are asking for concessions such as secondary tariffs and bans on Chinese goods, according to the report.

The push includes pressuring nations like Mexico to raise tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles, and lobbying countries in Europe and Asia to avoid becoming backdoor entry points for Chinese exports, Bloomberg wrote.

US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is spearheading the talks, aiming to form a united front to isolate Beijing economically, according to the report. But some allies remain wary, questioning both the strategy's success and the US' reliability.

A cohesive anti-China trade bloc remains uncertain, amid mixed signals from Washington and strained global ties, Bloomberg wrote.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Iron ore gains as focus shifts to favourable fundamentals
data.description:
Iron ore futures drifted higher on Thursday, as the focus shifted back to favourable fundamentals of firm near-term demand and lower supply, although intense trade tensions between the world's two largest economies capped gains.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) traded 0.99% higher at 713.5 yuan ($97.63) a metric ton as of 0256 GMT.

Earlier in the session, the contract hit 715 yuan, its highest since April 11.

The benchmark May iron ore (SZZFK5) on the Singapore Exchange was 0.57% higher at $98.75 a ton.

Inclement weather-induced lower supply in the past quarter, coupled with remaining firm near-term demand, supported prices of the key steelmaking ingredient.

BHP Group reported slightly lower third-quarter iron ore output due to cyclones. The other two giant miners Rio Tinto , and Vale reported lower first-quarter shipments and output, respectively.

Meanwhile, daily crude steel output among member steel mills was around 2.2 million tons in the first ten days in April, 3.4% higher than the prior ten-day period, data from the state-backed China Iron and Steel Association showed.

China's crude steel output in March jumped 4.6% to a ten-month high of 92.84 million metric tons.

Additionally, China's openness to trade talks helped boost sentiment across the commodity complex, ANZ analysts said in a note.

China on Wednesday unexpectedly named a new trade negotiator, a key appointment that some market participants interpreted as a positive signal to potentially ease the escalating tariff war with the United States.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE moved sideways, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! down 0.98% and coke (DCJcv1) up 0.93%.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange were mixed. Rebar ticked 0.1% higher, wire rod (SWRcv1) added 0.3%, stainless steel advanced 0.27% while hot-rolled coil shed 0.22%.

($1 = 7.3085 Chinese yuan)

data.title: U.S. Tariff Policy Poses Major Risk to Japan's Economy, BOJ Member Says
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

U.S. trade policy is one of the biggest risks to Japan's economic outlook, Bank of Japan board member Junko Nakagawa warned as she flagged continued uncertainty over tariffs.

"Since the extent of the [economic] impact largely depends on the future course of tariff policies, it is necessary to monitor developments with high vigilance," Nakagawa said in a speech to business leaders in Gunma prefecture, located in the center of Japan's main island.

Given the unpredictability of ongoing trade negotiations between the U.S. and Japan, many economists expect the BOJ to maintain its policy rate at 0.5% on May 1. ​In a Truth Social post earlier, Trump said it was a ​"Great Honor to have just met with the Japanese Delegation on Trade​," adding: ​"Big Progress!"

Japan's economy minister, who is in Washington for the talks, has said he is ready to listen to any requests, urging a "win-win" solution.

Nakagawa said higher U.S. tariffs will have a direct impact on Japanese corporate activity, negatively affecting exports, production, sales, capital expenditure and profits.

Trade restrictions could also weigh on the Japanese economy indirectly by deteriorating sentiment among businesses and households, or leading to wide fluctuations in commodity prices and financial markets, she added.

That view is in line with comments from BOJ Gov. Kazuo Ueda, who was testifying at a parliamentary committee meeting Thursday morning.

Signs of weakness in the economy could force the BOJ to pause, or slow, its attempt to raise interest rates further. On Thursday, Nakagawa reaffirmed the central bank's stance that it will continue to seek rate hikes if Japan's economy and price growth develop in line with projections.

For now, wage and price trends point to relatively bright domestic conditions in Japan. Major companies are giving employees another big pay raise this year.

"Upward pressure on wages could intensify further with labor market conditions remaining tight, and moves to reflect the past inflation and higher wages in selling prices could strengthen to a greater extent than expected," Nakagawa said.

Policymakers hope that wage growth will help improve sentiment among consumers, which has weakened due to persistent inflation.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: RBNZ's own preferred inflation model 2.9% for Q1 2025 (prior was 3.1% y/y in Q4 2024)
data.description:
Earlier today we had the official CPI data from NZ:

  • New Zealand Q1 CPI +2.5% y/y (expected +2.3%) & +0.9% q/q (expected +0.7%)

From the Reserve Bank of New Zealand now, its model falling below Q4 and under the top of its 3% band:

***

Reserve Bank of New Zealand's sectoral factor model of inflation, its preferred measure.

  • The model is based on the idea that inflation in each sector of the economy is influenced by a common set of underlying factors or "factors" such as changes in interest rates, exchange rates, and commodity prices.
  • The model describes how inflation in each sector of the economy is affected by the underlying factors. Estimates are derived from data on past inflation rates and other relevant indicators and are used to generate forecasts for future inflation in each sector.
  • The RBNZ sectoral factor model of inflation is particularly useful because it allows the central bank to identify the sources of inflationary pressures in different parts of the economy. For example, if inflation is rising rapidly in the housing sector, the RBNZ can use the model to determine whether this is due to changes in interest rates or other factors specific to the housing market. By understanding the sources of inflationary pressures, the RBNZ can adjust its monetary policy to target inflation effectively and achieve its inflation targets.
